channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Winnipeg reports decline in fires linked to homelessness factors in 2026
item.description: A report from Winnipeg indicates a notable reduction in fires during 2026, with authorities attributing this trend to the influence of homelessness as a contributing factor. The analysis suggests that...

item.title: Government Adjusts Fuel Prices Amid Global Oil Market Fluctuations
item.description: The government has raised petrol prices by Rs3.66 and high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs4.80 per litre, reflecting global oil price volatility due to renewed US-Iran tensions in the Persian Gulf. Petrol no...

item.title: Data Center Outage Disrupts Regional Power Grid
item.description: A recent incident involving a temporary power outage at a major data center in Data Center Alley has caused disruptions to the regional power grid. The outage, which lasted approximately 15 minutes, a...

item.title: Foreign National Collects Plastic Bottles in Nairobi, Sparks Public Discussion
item.description: A video of a foreign man collecting discarded plastic bottles on Nairobi streets has gone viral, sparking mixed reactions on social media. The man, dressed in a red shirt, black trousers, and a reflec...

item.title: Marais Erasmus: From Fiery Bowler to Umpire, Navigating Cricket's Challenges
item.description: The article chronicles the career of Marais Erasmus, a former South African cricket bowler turned umpire, highlighting his early days as a fiery fast bowler who took wickets during the New Zealand tou...

item.title: New Study Suggests Humans Could Live Up to 156 Years by Addressing DNA Damage
item.description: A new study published in npj Aging suggests humans may live up to 156 years if scientists can tackle somatic mutations, which are DNA damages that build up over time. The research highlights that the ...

item.title: Strong Group Signs Boban Marjanovic for Jones Cup 3-Peat Ambition
item.description: Strong Group, the Philippine basketball team aiming for three consecutive William Jones Cup titles, has signed former NBA player Boban Marjanovic as their first confirmed player. The 7-foot-4 Serbian ...

item.title: Dangote Refinery Secures $2.5bn in Private Investment for Expansion
item.description: Nigeria's Dangote Refinery has raised $2.5bn from private investors, marking a significant step towards its expansion plans and potential IPO. The funding will support doubling the refinery's capacity...

item.title: Paulo Avelino discusses his family-friendly business venture and investment philosophy
item.description: Paulo Avelino, the actor and entrepreneur, shares insights about his new business venture, Play Space, a family-friendly indoor playground. The article highlights his philosophy of investing in busine...

item.title: Severe Tropical Storm Kiyapo Intensifies; PAGASA Issues Forecast Updates for July 24, 2026
item.description: The Philippines' 11th tropical cyclone of 2026, Kiyapo, has intensified into a severe tropical storm, prompting PAGASA to issue updated forecasts for July 24, 2026. The storm, which made landfall in C...

item.title: Aliko Dangote's Expansion into Oil and Fertilizers: Building a N100 Billion Empire
item.description: Aliko Dangote, Africa's richest man, is scaling his business empire through a massive refinery and fertilizer projects. His plan aims to boost revenues from N20 billion to over N80 billion in three ye...

item.title: Government to Develop Chemical Parks Under BHAVYA-Rasayan Initiative
item.description: The Indian government has announced plans to establish three chemical parks under the BHAVYA-Rasayan initiative, aimed at boosting industrial growth and creating employment opportunities. These parks ...

item.title: Trump's Refugee Policy Favoring White South Africans Sparks Controversy
item.description: The article discusses the Trump administration's refugee policy that prioritizes white South Africans, raising concerns about racial bias and the politicization of refugee admissions. Experts criticiz...

item.title: Magomed Ankalaev Reflects on Defeat to Alex Pereira and His Comeback Journey
item.description: Magomed Ankalaev, the former UFC light heavyweight champion, is preparing for his return to the octagon after a significant loss to Alex Pereira. The defeat, which ended his 14-fight unbeaten streak, ...

item.title: TekSavvy and Independent Carriers Seek Lower Wholesale Rates to Boost Competition
item.description: TekSavvy, along with other independent internet providers and industry associations, has submitted an application to the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) requesting a...

item.title: Man confesses HIV infection to girlfriend via cake, sparks online debate
item.description: A man ordered a cake for his girlfriend that carried a shocking message admitting he had infected her with HIV. The cake, created by Tanzanian baker Cakefairy TZ, featured the text 'I'm sorry I have i...

item.title: Anthropic Launches Cost-Effective AI Model with Enhanced Performance
item.description: Anthropic, a leading AI startup competing with OpenAI and Google, has introduced Claude Opus 5, a new AI model that offers comparable performance to its most advanced system at half the cost. The mode...

item.title: Queensland records first suspected H5 bird flu case, raising concerns for native wildlife
item.description: Queensland has detected its first suspected case of highly pathogenic H5 avian influenza in a seabird, marking the virus's arrival on the state's coastline. While poultry products remain safe to consu...

item.title: India faces increased coal dependence due to El Niño impact on hydropower
item.description: The article discusses how El Niño conditions are causing a decline in hydropower generation in India, forcing the country to rely more on coal-fired power. S&P Global analysis shows a 6.3 aGW drop in ...

item.title: Irish Police Intercept Bomb Intended for Northern Ireland Attack
item.description: Irish police have intercepted a car containing an 'extremely significant' bomb near the border with Northern Ireland, preventing a potential attack. The vehicle was stopped during an operation targeti...
